cards.back: A linear layout is a type of layout in Android where the child views are arranged in a linear sequence, either horizontally or vertically.
An absolute layout is a type of layout in Android where child views are positioned relative to their parent layout, rather than relative to each other.
A frame is a type of layout in Android that provides a basic structure for holding content, such as a layout or an adapter view.
Linear layout is used when the child views need to be arranged in a sequence, such as in a list or a button row.
Absolute layout is used when the child views need to be positioned relative to each other, such as in a dialog box or a popup menu.
